title: chabertia agrestis
intro: Chabertia agrestis, also known as wild celery or cow parsley, is a biennial plant that belongs to the parsley family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its essential oils, which are known to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Chabertia agrestis include relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and aiding in digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and menstrual disorders.
